full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Choosing which FGM components to ablate
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: For the FGM paper (Tan et al., sparse SVM feature selection), I need to ablate two distinct components. I've read Algorithm 1 and Section 2.2 — which two components would be most meaningful to ablate so I can show what the method actually relies on? I want to pick things that are clearly defined in the paper, not generic SVM stuff.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Suggested the cutting-plane iteration count (Section 2.7 says 'about 10 iterations') and the sparsity budget B. I had already thought of B but hadn't tied MAX_ITER to Section 2.7; I went back and confirmed that sentence in the paper and used both for my two ablations.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Section 2.7 explicitly states a maximum of 10 iterations is enough for convergence. Section 2 and 3.2 define B as the number of features in the mask. Both are ablated independently in my notebook.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: The Part B instructions say each ablation must be independent — when testing one component, keep all others at full settings. For FGM, if I ablate MAX_ITER, should I fix B at some value and only change the number of iterations? And for the B ablation, fix MAX_ITER at 10? I want to make sure I'm doing it correctly.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Confirmed that for Ablation 1 I should fix B (e.g. B=5) and vary MAX_ITER; for Ablation 2 fix MAX_ITER=10 and vary B. I implemented exactly that in the notebook.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Re-read the Task 3.1 wording: 'when testing one component, keep all other parts of the method at their full settings.' My code uses B=5 for the MAX_ITER ablation and MAX_ITER=10 for the B ablation.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: My MAX_ITER ablation on Ionosphere shows test accuracy and sparsity changing as I go from 1 to 10 iterations. With only 1 iteration the working set never grows. How should I explain in 5–7 sentences why this matters for FGM — what does the paper say about convergence and when does the method 'stop'?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Clarified that with one iteration FGM behaves like single-shot selection and that convergence is when the new d_hat is already in the working set (Section 2.2). I wrote the interpretation paragraph myself, tying it to Algorithm 1 and the 10-iteration remark in 2.7.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Section 2.2 describes the cutting-plane loop and when to add a new mask. My markdown cell cites Section 2.2 and 2.7 and states that truncating iterations is a real ablation of the core loop.

full_llm_interaction_log.purpose: Plot and table for B ablation
full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: For the B (sparsity budget) ablation I have a table of B vs test accuracy and sparsity. Should I plot accuracy vs B and sparsity vs B on the same figure or separate? I want the visualisation to clearly show the trade-off the paper talks about.
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Suggested either two subplots or one plot with two y-axes. I went with a single plot of test accuracy vs B and included the sparsity values in the table so the trade-off is visible without cluttering the figure.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: The plot is saved to partB/results/task_3_1_ablation_B.png. The table in the notebook shows both Test_Acc_% and Sparsity for each B so the interpretation can reference the trade-off.

full_llm_interaction_log.prompt: The rubric says for each ablation I need a paragraph (5–7 sentences) interpreting the result — whether removing the component affected performance, whether the size of the change matches my expectation, and what it reveals about that component. Can you give me a checklist so I don't miss any of these when I write it?
full_llm_interaction_log.how_it_helped: Gave a short checklist: (1) effect on performance, (2) size of change vs expectation, (3) what it reveals about the component. I used that to structure both interpretation paragraphs and then wrote them in my own words from my actual numbers.
full_llm_interaction_log.student_verification: Both markdown interpretation cells in the notebook address effect on performance, whether the change matched expectation, and what the ablation reveals. I did not copy any text; I wrote them after looking at my plots and the paper.

top_5_prompts.why_important: Confirmed the independent-ablation setup so I could implement it correctly: fix B=5 for MAX_ITER ablation and MAX_ITER=10 for B ablation.

top_5_prompts.why_important: Clarified convergence and single-shot behaviour; I used that to write my own interpretation linking to Algorithm 1 and Section 2.7.

student_declaration.statement: I declare that this JSON file contains a complete and honest record of my LLM usage for Task 3.1. I used the LLM to decide which components to ablate, to confirm the independent-ablation setup, and to structure my interpretation paragraphs; I then verified each point against the paper and wrote all explanations and code myself.
